name: chord-chips-accessible
note: P3-2/P3-5 (P0) + P3 UAT regression/taste fixes (PR #300 follow-up). Base a11y claim, RED-FIRST vs pre-S-CHORDCHIP-A11Y main: chips render as <span> (not BUTTON, no tabIndex), carry no aria-label. UAT fix 1 (regression): <button> chips resubjected chord-play to the mobile click-delay - play must fire on pointerdown (before/without a click), and a click that follows a pointerdown must NOT replay (double-sound guard), while a keyboard-only click (no preceding pointerdown) still plays exactly once. UAT fix 2 (taste): the old persistent '.chordHint' row is retired for a one-shot Notable banner under the chip row - reclaims the row once dismissed/re-shown.

steps.label: every chord chip is a real, keyboard-focusable control (native <button>, tabIndex >= 0)
every chord chip has a non-empty accessible name that names the chord
steps.js: Array.prototype.every.call(document.querySelectorAll('.chordChips .c'), function(e){ var l = e.getAttribute('aria-label'); return !!l && l.trim().length > 0 && l.indexOf(e.textContent.trim()) >= 0; })

steps.label: chip has touch-action:manipulation (drops the mobile double-tap-zoom click delay)
S-CHORDCHIP-LAG: a bare pointerdown (no click) plays the chord immediately - no wait for the browser's follow-up click
S-CHORDCHIP-LAG: the browser's follow-up click after a pointerdown on the SAME chip does not replay it (no double-sound)
keyboard activation (click with no preceding pointerdown - the Enter/Space path) still plays exactly once
the tap-to-hear cue is a dismissible one-shot Notable (has its own close control), not a permanent row
